posts.author: Ketan_Chheda
posts.community: Neetu Yoshi Ltd - Railways component manufacturer
posts.text: Hi, The company raised 27.48Cr by issuing warrants for working capital needs of the track section, and the justification given is that they want to remain debt free, and dont want to bear the interest cost in order to keep their overall costs lower. How reasonable is that? Is this minorty shareholder friendly?

posts.author: Amnesiac
posts.community: Neetu Yoshi Ltd - Railways component manufacturer
posts.text: This is why their tax rates are lower. Apart from the lower tax rates these are some of the points which contribute to their OPM as per the management. I don’t rule out any accounting practices here, that’s why my view is very short term. As mentioned,my conviction about the management is not 100%. Invested a small portion hoping they should be able to meet their target of 110Cr sales for FY26, they have executed in the first half as per guidance.

posts.author: jagadishsabat
posts.community: Neetu Yoshi Ltd - Railways component manufacturer
posts.text: Hi @Amnesiac thanks for this post on Neetu Yoshi. My view on Neetu Yoshi is below - The OPM seems to be very high and not sustainable at current range of 33%, it should come down to in the range of 22-25%. Tax rate seems low at 18%. it should be around 25%. Both of the above seems like using accounting advantage to boost the valuation for IPO. seems like another typical case. Post the above correction the valuation is likely to drop. On the overall long run basis, it seems like a good idea.
